scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE Civil Judge (Senior Division)
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute this suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued. The Defendant resides at Koramangala, Bengaluru, and the cause of action arose within Bengaluru where the loan was advanced and repayment was demanded. Hence, this Court has jurisdiction under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. The suit is filed in the Court of the lowest grade competent to try it, and the suit value is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court.

LIMITATION

2. The loan being repayable on demand, the suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963. The demand for repayment was made within the statutory period, and the suit is timely instituted.

FACTS OF THE TRANSACTION

3. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a hand loan of Rs. 15,00,000 to the Defendant. The amount was transferred via NEFT bank transfer from the Plaintiff's account to the Defendant's account, bearing UTR Number AXIB20240315123456. There was no written loan agreement executed between the parties; however, the Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the loan amount via WhatsApp messages. The transaction took place in Bengaluru, and the repayment was due at the Plaintiff's residence in Jayanagar, Bengaluru. The bank transaction record is marked as Annexure A.

DEFAULT AND DEMAND

4. The loan was advanced on the understanding that it would be repaid on demand. The Defendant failed to repay the amount despite the Plaintiff's repeated oral demands. The Plaintiff sent a legal notice to the Defendant dated 01.07.2024 demanding repayment of the outstanding amount. The Defendant received the said notice but failed to respond or repay the loan amount. The legal notice is marked as Annexure C.

QUANTIFICATION OF CLAIM

5. The Plaintiff claims recovery of the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000. The Plaintiff is entitled to interest at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of default, i.e., 20.06.2024, till the date of realisation. The interest claimed is simple interest as there was no written agreement providing for compound interest. The total outstanding as on the date of filing comprises the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000 along with accrued interest calculated from 20.06.2024. The WhatsApp messages acknowledging the loan are marked as Annexure B.

LEGAL BASIS

6. The Plaintiff is entitled to recovery under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, as the Defendant has committed a breach of the obligation to repay the lent amount. The loan was advanced via banking channel, establishing clear consideration and negating any defence of lack of consideration. The suit is within limitation as the cause of action arose recently in Bengaluru.

7. The rate of interest claimed at 12% per annum is reasonable and within market norms for unsecured lending, not usurious. The Plaintiff has not received any part-payment towards the principal amount, and the entire sum remains outstanding.

CAUSE OF ACTION

8. The cause of action first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant failed to repay the loan amount despite the demand implied by the date of default. The Defendant has still failed to repay the amount. The suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed by law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

9. The total suit valuation is Rs. 15,00,000 (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only) for the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee. The court fee paid is {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} computed under the {{COURT_FEE_ACT_NAME}}. The plaint is properly stamped and tendered with the requisite court fee as required by law.

INTEREST

10. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pre-suit interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from {{DATE_OF_DEFAULT}} (date of default) until the date of institution of the suit;
    (b) Pendente lite interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of institution of the suit until the date of decree, under Order XX Rule 11 CPC;
    (c) Post-decree interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of decree until realization of the entire decretal amount, under Section 34 CPC.

PRAYER

11. In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:
    (a) Pass a decree for recovery of Rs. 15,00,000 (Rupees Fiftee
